vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The expected specimens (P1, P2, P3, P4) are not visible as living, identifiable botanical structures. The desk surface contains only empty pots, soil, and debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No foliage present in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No foliage present in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: No foliage present in black pot; white rabbit anchor is absent.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark and potentially desiccated or overly compacted.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely crushed eggshells) on the surface of the black pot, which remains consistent throughout the sequence.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no biological growth or secondary sprouts observed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, non-living environment. There is no growth, no movement, and no recovery. The 'white rabbit' anchor is missing from the start of the provided sequence, suggesting the loss occurred prior to the first image.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered plants have suffered total systemic loss. The environment is devoid of photosynthetic activity.
anomalies: Persistent presence of white shell-like fragments in the black pot.
Absence of the scale anchor (white rabbit) throughout the entire observed timeline.
Presence of a metallic spoon in the yellow pot (T-5) which was later removed.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms that the botanical registry is currently mismatched with physical reality. The pots are present but contain no living plant matter. The environment appears to have been abandoned or suffered a total collapse of all specimens prior to the start of this observation window. No signs of recovery or new growth are present.
